guideline_references: RANZCO/optometry guidance on the acute red/painful eye
intentional_test_features: Red herrings: severe bilateral sicca with eye pain and dysphagia mimics corneal ulcer/keratitis, bacterial parotitis and oesophageal obstruction.
Important-not-to-miss: sight-threatening corneal ulceration and bacterial parotitis must be screened; the cornea can't be examined remotely.
Anchoring: 'known Sjogren flare' should not close off a corneal ulcer or bacterial parotitis.
Commission trap: intensive lubrication and continuing the usual NSAID are reasonable, but unproven agents (tadalafil/LDN) should not be presented as therapy.
Telehealth limits: slit-lamp/fluorescein ocular examination and parotid/joint palpation cannot be done over chat.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date)
